metadata.title: 11th Pass Debug Factorial — Vascadia v0.2-beta, Post-Infrastructure-Fix, europe-west4
metadata.priority: P0 — validates that ALL infrastructure fixes from this session actually work in production
metadata.estimated-cost: ~$6-10 GCP L4 (spot for DynUNet/zero-shot, on-demand for SAM3)

change.description: ALL GCP infrastructure migrated from europe-north1 (Finland) to europe-west4 (Netherlands). Zero resources remain in the old region. Verified via FinOps snapshot.
change.details: GAR docker registry: europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess GCS buckets: all 3 in europe-west4 (dvc-data, mlflow-artifacts, checkpoints) Cloud SQL: mlflow-db-479b793 in europe-west4 Cloud Run MLflow: minivess-mlflow in europe-west4 GPU VMs: SkyPilot launches in europe-west4 (co-located = zero egress)

change.description: MLflow 413 "Request Entity Too Large" FIXED. Cloud Run MLflow deployed with gs:// artifact root pointing to gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/. RUNTIME VERIFIED — artifacts upload directly to GCS, bypassing the Cloud Run proxy entirely. No more 32 MB request size limit.
change.details: Image: europe-west4-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/mlflow:v3.10.0 Artifact root: gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts Cloud SQL backend: mlflow-db-479b793 (PostgreSQL 15) URL: https://minivess-mlflow-a7w6hliydq-ez.a.run.app
change.verification: Phase 1 J1 (DynUNet) will verify 68 MB checkpoint uploads succeed. Phase 1 J2 (SAM3 Hybrid) will verify ~900 MB checkpoint uploads succeed.

change.description: file_mounts REMOVED from ALL SkyPilot YAMLs. MLflow GCS artifact store is now the ONLY persistence mechanism for checkpoints and artifacts. This eliminates the competing persistence anti-pattern that caused months of confusion (see metalearning: 2026-03-27-mlflow-413-10-passes-never-fixed).
change.details: Removed: file_mounts to gs://minivess-mlops-checkpoints Contract: mlflow_only_artifact_contract (knowledge-graph invariant) If MLflow breaks: FIX MLflow, do NOT add file_mounts back.

change.description: SkyPilot jobs controller noted at n4-standard-4 in europe-west1-b. The controller orchestrates but does not transfer data. Its location does not affect egress costs. Downsize to e2-medium is a future optimization (not yet applied as of this plan).
change.cost-impact: Current: ~EUR 93-100/month (n4-standard-4). Potential: ~EUR 15-20/month (e2-medium) if SkyPilot supports it.

change.description: region_config changed from europe_us to europe_strict in debug.yaml. Single-region europe-west4 only — zero cross-region egress, zero US fallback. Appropriate for debug runs where cost predictability matters more than availability.
change.details: Config file: configs/cloud/regions/europe_strict.yaml Regions: europe-west4 only (Netherlands, 3 zones) No US fallback — if europe-west4 has no L4 capacity, jobs PEND.

change.description: 107 new tests added during this session across multiple categories: double-check tests, FinOps validation, experiment harness, config scanner.
Docker base image rebuilt and pushed to europe-west4 GAR. Image includes all fixes from this session.
All stale SkyPilot jobs (IDs 108-157) cancelled. Clean job queue.

root-causes.cause: MLflow 413 — checkpoint upload exceeds Cloud Run 32 MB proxy limit. Training succeeds but ALL artifacts (checkpoints, metrics) are lost.
SAM3 OOM in check_gradient_flow() — gradient materialization with gradient_checkpointing=true causes CUDA OOM on L4 (24 GB).
L4 spot preemption rate ~80% in europe-west4 for SAM3 jobs (25+ min duration). 3 recoveries then FAILED.
Cross-region GAR egress (europe-north1 to europe-west4) adding latency and EUR 89/month cost.
file_mounts competing with MLflow for artifact persistence — created confusion about which mechanism was actually working.
VesselFM zero-shot: 3 FAILED_SETUP attempts — HF download or DVC pull issues.
Job #154 ran 12+ hours — training completed in 5 min but post-training subflow + spot preemption + 413 = 12h wasted.

fixes-applied.fix: MLflow Cloud Run redeployed with gs:// artifact root — artifacts upload directly to GCS, no proxy limit. Runtime verified.
skip_gradient_flow guard when gradient_checkpointing=true. Already present from 10th pass pre-run fixes.
SAM3 jobs use ON-DEMAND (not spot) to avoid preemption. 80% preemption on 25+ min jobs is economically irrational.
ALL infrastructure migrated to europe-west4. Same-region = zero egress. Verified via FinOps snapshot.
file_mounts REMOVED from all SkyPilot YAMLs. MLflow is the ONLY persistence mechanism.
VesselFM setup: retry logic with timeout + exit code 33/34 for EAGER_NEXT_REGION recovery.
Monitoring protocol with max_duration alarms prevents 12h runaways.

qa-improvements-since-prior-pass.improvement: 107 new tests: double-check, FinOps, harness, config scanner
GCS bucket consistency tests (cross-file name matching)

previous-pass-results.model: All training conditions passed. Checkpoints LOST due to 413 (saved only via file_mounts workaround which is now removed). MUST re-verify that artifact upload works via the new MLflow GCS path.
All training conditions passed. Same checkpoint loss caveat as DynUNet. MUST re-verify artifact upload.
All 20 attempts FAILED. Root causes: OOM in check_gradient_flow() + 80% spot preemption rate. Fixes applied: gradient flow skip + on-demand pricing.
Job #154 ran 12+ hours. Training likely completed in 5 min but post-training + spot + 413 = 12h wasted. All conditions must re-run.
3 attempts, all FAILED_SETUP. HF download or DVC pull issues. Retry logic added with exit codes 33/34 for recovery.

step.name: Branch and Environment
step.description: Ensure you are on the correct branch with all fixes.

step.name: Environment Variables
step.description: Verify .env has the correct values for GCP Cloud Run MLflow. The MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI MUST point to the Cloud Run URL, NOT localhost.

required-env-vars.var: Your HuggingFace token (required for SAM3 + VesselFM weight download)

step.warning: If MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI=http://localhost:5000, ALL cloud job metrics/artifacts will be LOST. The training VM cannot reach your local Docker MLflow.

step.name: Verify GCP Authentication
step.description: SkyPilot and DVC need GCP Application Default Credentials.

step.name: Verify Docker Image on GAR
step.description: The base image MUST exist in europe-west4 GAR and be fresh (built after the latest commit with fixes).

step.name: Verify DVC Data on GCS
step.description: MiniVess training data MUST be on GCS for cloud VMs to pull. If buckets are empty, push data first.
step.warning: If the bucket is empty (0 bytes), data has not been pushed yet. Push with: dvc push -r gcs The data MUST be there before launching — cloud VMs pull from GCS.

step.name: Verify MLflow Connectivity
step.description: The Cloud Run MLflow server MUST be reachable and using GCS artifact store.

commands.cmd: uv run python -c " import mlflow mlflow.set_tracking_uri('https://minivess-mlflow-a7w6hliydq-ez.a.run.app') client = mlflow.tracking.MlflowClient() exps = client.search_experiments(max_results=1) print(f'MLflow OK: {len(exps)} experiments') "

step.criterion: HTTP 200 from health endpoint, MLflow client connects

step.name: Verify SkyPilot Controller
step.description: The SkyPilot jobs controller must be running. If stopped, start it.

commands.cmd: uv run sky jobs queue 2>&1 | head -5

step.note: The controller is in europe-west1-b (n4-standard-4). This is expected — it orchestrates jobs but does not transfer data. Training VMs are in europe-west4 (co-located with GAR/GCS/Cloud SQL).

step.name: Run Test Suite
step.description: All tests MUST pass before launching. Zero tolerance for pre-existing failures.

step.name: Run Preflight and Dry Run
step.description: Preflight validates GCP infrastructure. Dry run validates factorial conditions.
step.criterion: Preflight: all checks pass. Dry run: correct condition count.

step.name: Create Report File BEFORE Launch
step.description: The report file MUST exist before launching (Rule H1: REPORT-BEFORE-LAUNCH). Create it with headers and empty tables.

monitoring-protocol.overview: Use /experiment-harness to orchestrate and /factorial-monitor to monitor. The harness creates the XML + report, validates gates, then drives /factorial-monitor for polling + diagnosis + selective relaunch.

expected-durations.job-type: DynUNet is the fastest model. 2 epochs on half data should complete in under 10 minutes. If it exceeds 30 minutes, something is wrong (setup stuck, DVC pull failing, MLflow connectivity issue).
MambaVesselNet is similar to DynUNet in training speed.
SAM3 TopoLoRA uses gradient checkpointing + BS=1 + grad_accum=4. Slower than DynUNet but should complete within 25 minutes.
SAM3 Hybrid: same profile as TopoLoRA. ~900 MB checkpoint upload adds 1-2 minutes vs DynUNet's 68 MB.
Zero-shot: no training, only evaluation. Should be fast. Includes model weight download (~9 GB) in setup.
Zero-shot: no training, only evaluation on DeepVess. Includes VesselFM weight download in setup.

duration-alarm-protocol.level: Log: "Job X at expected duration — normal."
Action: Tail job logs via `sky jobs logs JOB_ID`. Look for: stuck DVC pull, hanging MLflow upload, spot recovery loop, no output for 10+ minutes.
Action: `sky jobs cancel JOB_ID`. The job is stuck — do not let it burn credits. Investigate root cause before relaunching.

per-poll-actions.action: Update report status matrix with current job states
Check for newly terminal jobs (SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELLED)
For each newly terminal failure: run ralph_monitor.analyze_logs()

log-tailing.trigger: No output from a job for 10 minutes

common-stuck-patterns.pattern: Stuck at "Pulling training data from GCS via DVC..." — DVC pull hanging
Stuck at "Pre-caching SAM3 weights..." — HF rate limited
Stuck at "Checking MLflow server connectivity..." — Cloud Run cold start
No output at all — setup script crashed before logging

cost-tracking.per-job: Track start_time, end_time, GPU type, spot vs on-demand. L4 spot: $0.2204/hr. L4 on-demand: $0.70/hr.
cost-tracking.running-total: Update after each terminal job: cumulative_cost += (duration_hours * hourly_rate)
cost-tracking.budget-alarm: If total cost exceeds $15, STOP and reassess. The debug factorial should cost $6-10 total. $15+ means something is wrong.

monitoring-protocol.kill-switch: 3+ identical failures within 5 minutes AND remaining running jobs have not passed the failure point → cancel same-config jobs and begin batch diagnosis. Different-config jobs continue.

skill-references.skill: Creates XML plan, validates gates, drives factorial-monitor, writes report, captures compound learning. 5 phases: GENERATE, VALIDATE, EXECUTE, COMPOUND, REFLECT.
Multi-job monitoring, aggregated diagnosis, selective relaunch. 6 phases: LAUNCH, MONITOR, DIAGNOSE, FIX, RELAUNCH, REPORT. Rules F1-F5 prevent known anti-patterns.
Per-job log analysis when a specific job fails. Used by factorial-monitor for individual failure diagnosis.

phase.name: Infrastructure Validation — 3 Focused Jobs
phase.description:
Before running any factorial conditions, validate that the infrastructure fixes actually work in production with 3 carefully chosen test jobs. Each tests a different code path and artifact size.
Use ON-DEMAND for SAM3 (no spot preemption during validation). Use SPOT for DynUNet (fast job, preemption recoverable).
CRITICAL: ALL 3 jobs must succeed before proceeding to Phase 2. If ANY fails, diagnose and fix BEFORE launching the factorial. Do NOT "just retry" — understand the failure.

verification.criterion: Training completes 2 epochs in under 10 min
Checkpoint appears in gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/ (NOT 413)
MLflow UI shows run with all training metrics and artifacts
No "HttpArtifactRepository" or "413" in job logs
DVC data pull from gs://minivess-mlops-dvc-data succeeds
Docker image pull from europe-west4 GAR completes without egress charges

job.name: sam3_hybrid-cbdice_cldice-calibfalse-f0
job.note: ON-DEMAND — no spot preemption during validation. ~$0.70/hr x 0.5h = ~$0.35

verification.criterion: SAM3 weights load successfully (ViT-32L, 648M params)
NaN guard fires on cbdice_cldice init (expected, not a bug)
Training completes 2 epochs without OOM
~900 MB checkpoint uploads to GCS without 413
VRAM peak below 16 GB (L4 has 24 GB)
No gradient_flow check (skipped due to gradient_checkpointing=true)
Post-training sub-flow completes (if applicable)
Zero-shot evaluation completes (no training, evaluation only)
eval/ metrics logged to MLflow without 413
SAM3 weight download succeeds (HuggingFace, ~9 GB)
DVC pull for MiniVess eval data succeeds

phase.gate: ALL 3 jobs MUST succeed before proceeding to Phase 2. Decision matrix: - J1 fails → MLflow GCS path is broken. Fix Cloud Run config. - J2 fails → SAM3 OOM or large checkpoint upload issue. Check VRAM + GCS. - J3 fails → Zero-shot code path or HF download issue. Check setup script. If ANY fails, diagnose root cause, fix, rebuild Docker if needed, re-run. Do NOT proceed to Phase 2 with any doubt about infrastructure.

phase.name: Failed Factorial Conditions — SAM3 + Zero-Shot
phase.description:
Run the 18 conditions that failed in passes 9-10. DynUNet (12/12) and MambaVesselNet (8/8) training succeeded — they are NOT re-run here. (Phase 3 handles artifact re-verification for those models.)
Strategy: - SAM3 (16 conditions): ON-DEMAND to avoid 80% spot preemption rate - Zero-shot (2 conditions): SPOT (short jobs, preemption recoverable)

cost-estimate.note: Compared to 10th pass: $23.30 wasted with zero useful output

monitoring.kill-switch: 3+ identical failures within 5 min → cancel all same-config jobs. Different-config jobs continue running.

phase.launch-notes:
SAM3 on-demand: IMPLEMENTED. debug.yaml model_overrides has use_spot: false for sam3_topolora and sam3_hybrid. run_factorial.sh reads this and passes --no-use-spot to sky jobs launch for those models. Verified via dry-run (2026-03-28): SAM3 shows spot=false + --no-use-spot flag, DynUNet/Mamba spot=true.
Phase 2 selective launch: run_factorial.sh launches ALL 32 conditions. Use --resume to skip already-SUCCEEDED/PENDING/RUNNING conditions from prior passes. Phase 1 J1 (DynUNet) will be in SUCCEEDED state, so --resume skips it automatically. For the 18 failed SAM3 conditions, all are new — they will launch fresh.
DeepVess gap: P2.18 (VesselFM zero-shot on DeepVess) requires DeepVess data on GCS which is NOT yet available. Launch P2.1-P2.17 first. Handle P2.18 after downloading DeepVess from Cornell eCommons and pushing to GCS.

phase.name: Re-verify Artifact Upload for DynUNet + MambaVesselNet
phase.description:
DynUNet and MambaVesselNet succeeded in training (20/20 conditions) but ALL checkpoints were LOST because: 1. MLflow 413 prevented upload via tracking server 2. file_mounts workaround saved to GCS but is now REMOVED 3. MLflow is now the ONLY persistence path
After the GCS artifact fix, we MUST verify that the MLflow upload path works for these models too. Run 1 condition each — we do NOT need full re-training, just enough to verify the checkpoint upload.
NOTE: If Phase 1 J1 (DynUNet) already verified artifact upload successfully, Phase 3 DynUNet can be SKIPPED. Only run MambaVesselNet.

condition.purpose: Verify 68 MB DynUNet checkpoint uploads to GCS via MLflow
condition.skip-if: Phase 1 J1 already verified DynUNet artifact upload

condition.purpose: Verify MambaVesselNet checkpoint uploads to GCS via MLflow

verification.criterion: Checkpoint appears in gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/
MLflow UI shows artifact with correct size
No 413 in job logs
Checkpoint can be downloaded: gsutil ls -la gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/...

factorial-design.summary: Full factorial: 4 x 4 x 2 x 2 x 2 x 5 = 640 conditions + 2 zero-shot = 642. Debug reduction: 1 fold (not 3), 2 epochs (not 50), half data. GPU jobs for this pass: 32 training + 2 zero-shot = 34 GPU jobs total. This pass runs ONLY the 18 failed conditions + 2 artifact-verification. Total jobs this pass: 3 (Phase 1) + 18 (Phase 2) + 1-2 (Phase 3) = 22-23 jobs.

watchlist.item: GCS artifact upload for large SAM3 checkpoints (~900 MB). The 413 is fixed but we have never successfully uploaded a 900 MB checkpoint to GCS via MLflow. Phase 1 J2 is the FIRST real test.

L4 spot availability in europe-west4. With europe_strict config, there is NO US fallback. If europe-west4 L4 capacity is exhausted, jobs PEND indefinitely. Monitor PENDING duration — if > 30 min, consider switching zero-shot jobs to on-demand.
VesselFM zero-shot setup: 3 consecutive FAILED_SETUP in 10th pass. Retry logic added but root cause (HF download or DVC pull?) never conclusively identified. Watch P2.18 closely.

DVC data on GCS buckets. The FinOps report shows ALL buckets are empty (0 bytes). If data has not been pushed with `dvc push -r gcs`, ALL jobs will fail at setup with exit code 33. Verify CS5 BEFORE launching.
SkyPilot API server version mismatch: server=1.0.0.dev20260314, client=1.0.0.dev20260326 (12 days behind). May cause subtle issues. Consider `sky api stop && sky api start` before launching.
Cloud SQL authorized_networks=0.0.0.0/0. Not a functional issue but a security risk. Should be tightened after experiment validation.
Untagged MLflow image in GAR (267 MiB). Cleanup candidate to save ~$0.03/month. Low priority.
MiniVess data pushed to GCS (211 files, 984 MB) on 2026-03-28. DVC cache populated via dvc commit download -f, then dvc push -r gcs.

DeepVess data NOT yet on GCS. Source: Cornell eCommons (not EBRAINS). VesselFM zero-shot (P2.18) will fail at DVC pull (exit 33) until pushed. Download from: https://ecommons.cornell.edu/items/a79bb6d8-77cf-4917-8e26-f2716a6ac2a3 Then: dvc commit data/raw/deepvess.dvc, dvc push -r gcs. WORKAROUND: Launch P2.1-P2.17 first, handle P2.18 after DeepVess download.
SkyPilot API server version mismatch resolved by restart (sky api stop/start) on 2026-03-28. Server now matches client v1.0.0.dev20260326.

council-review.summary: 5-domain expert agents reviewed this plan independently. Overall scores: Infrastructure 7/10, FinOps 8/10, Monitoring 6/10, Scientific Rigor 8/10, DevEx 5/10. Three CRITICAL issues found and fixed.

critical-fixes-applied.fix: CS1 stale repo path: /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/vascadia corrected to /home/petteri/Dropbox/knowledge-hub/repositories/vascadia

SAM3 on-demand mechanism: Added use_spot: false to debug.yaml model_overrides for sam3_topolora and sam3_hybrid. run_factorial.sh now reads this and passes --no-use-spot to sky jobs launch. Verified via dry-run: SAM3 shows spot=false while DynUNet/MambaVesselNet remain spot=true.
GCS data: MiniVess pushed (211 files, 984 MB). DeepVess NOT yet pushed — download from Cornell eCommons required. P2.18 deferred until available.

council-review.cost-corrections: L4 on-demand: $0.74/hr (plan said $0.70/hr, delta +6%). SAM3 duration: 30 min realistic estimate (plan said 25 min aspirational). Corrected Phase 2 SAM3 cost: 16 x 30 min x $0.74/hr = ~$5.92 (plan: $4.67). Corrected total: ~$6.53 (plan: $5.30, delta +23%). Budget cap $15 still has 2.3x headroom. APPROVED. RECOMMENDATION: Two-tier budget alarm — $8 WARNING + $15 STOP.

monitoring-gaps.gap: Monitoring exists only as XML declarations. ralph_monitor.py has zero duration-checking logic. Duration alarms, kill-switch, and cost tracking depend entirely on the Claude Code session maintaining coherence for 2+ hours.
RECOMMENDATION: Use Phase 1 as calibration — record actual durations from J1/J2/J3 and update warn/cancel thresholds before Phase 2.
Reduce SAM3 CANCEL threshold from 5x (125 min) to 3x (75 min). At on-demand rates, 125 min cancel = $1.54/job wasted. 16 jobs = $24.64 worst case.

scientific-observations.observation: Rule 27 compliance EXCELLENT — debug.yaml identical to paper_full.yaml factors
SWAG correctly defined as Maddox et al. 2019 (not checkpoint averaging)
VesselFM data leakage correctly handled (DeepVess only)
RECOMMENDATION: Add SAM3 Vanilla zero-shot on DeepVess for direct comparison (~$0.06)

devex-improvements-needed.improvement: Add explicit Phase 1 launch commands (see phase1-launch-commands below)
Document selective launch mechanism for Phase 2 (18 of 32 conditions)

phase1-launch-commands.description: These are the EXACT commands to launch Phase 1 validation jobs. Run from the repo root. Requires: .env with MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI + HF_TOKEN, SkyPilot configured (sky check gcp), Docker image on GAR, DVC data on GCS.

job.name: DynUNet baseline + GCS artifact verify
job.command: uv run sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml \ --name dynunet-dice_ce-calibfalse-f0 \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=dynunet \ --env LOSS_NAME=dice_ce \ --env FOLD_ID=0 \ --env WITH_AUX_CALIB=false \ --env MAX_EPOCHS=2 \ --env MAX_TRAIN_VOLUMES=23 \ --env MAX_VAL_VOLUMES=12 \ --env EXPERIMENT_NAME=debug_factorial \ --env ZERO_SHOT=false \ --env EVAL_DATASET=minivess \ --env POST_TRAINING_METHODS=none,swag \ --env BATCH_SIZE=2 \ --env GRAD_ACCUM_STEPS=1 \ --env GRADIENT_CHECKPOINTING=false \ --env-file .env \ -y

job.name: SAM3 Hybrid large checkpoint + GCS upload
job.command: uv run sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml \ --name sam3_hybrid-cbdice_cldice-calibfalse-f0 \ --no-use-spot \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_hybrid \ --env LOSS_NAME=cbdice_cldice \ --env FOLD_ID=0 \ --env WITH_AUX_CALIB=false \ --env MAX_EPOCHS=2 \ --env MAX_TRAIN_VOLUMES=23 \ --env MAX_VAL_VOLUMES=12 \ --env EXPERIMENT_NAME=debug_factorial \ --env ZERO_SHOT=false \ --env EVAL_DATASET=minivess \ --env POST_TRAINING_METHODS=none,swag \ --env BATCH_SIZE=1 \ --env GRAD_ACCUM_STEPS=4 \ --env GRADIENT_CHECKPOINTING=true \ --env-file .env \ -y

job.name: SAM3 Vanilla zero-shot path verify
job.command: uv run sky jobs launch deployment/skypilot/train_factorial.yaml \ --name sam3_vanilla-zeroshot-minivess-f0 \ --env MODEL_FAMILY=sam3_vanilla \ --env LOSS_NAME=none \ --env FOLD_ID=0 \ --env WITH_AUX_CALIB=false \ --env MAX_EPOCHS=0 \ --env MAX_TRAIN_VOLUMES=23 \ --env MAX_VAL_VOLUMES=12 \ --env EXPERIMENT_NAME=debug_factorial \ --env ZERO_SHOT=true \ --env EVAL_DATASET=minivess \ --env POST_TRAINING_METHODS=none \ --env BATCH_SIZE=1 \ --env GRAD_ACCUM_STEPS=1 \ --env GRADIENT_CHECKPOINTING=false \ --env-file .env \ -y

phase1-launch-commands.gate: ALL 3 jobs MUST reach SUCCEEDED before proceeding to Phase 2. After each job completes, verify: - gsutil ls gs://minivess-mlops-mlflow-artifacts/ (artifacts uploaded) - sky jobs logs JOB_ID | grep -c "413" (must be 0) - Record ACTUAL duration for Phase 2 threshold calibration: J1 actual: __ min (expected 10 min, warn 30, cancel 50) J2 actual: __ min (expected 25 min, warn 75, cancel 75) J3 actual: __ min (expected 15 min, warn 45, cancel 75) - If J1_actual > 15 min: multiply all Phase 2 thresholds by J1_actual/10

success-criteria.criterion: ZERO "413" or "Request Entity Too Large" in any job log
Total cost below $15 (budget for debug run including all phases)
MLflow UI shows all runs with metrics AND artifacts
Phase 3 artifact verification succeeds for MambaVesselNet
Zero cross-region egress charges in GCP billing (next-day check)

what-constitutes-failure.failure: Any job with 413 error → MLflow GCS fix did not work
SAM3 OOM → gradient checkpointing or VRAM guard broken
DVC pull failure on all jobs → data not on GCS
Single job timeout → spot preemption or setup hang
VesselFM FAILED_SETUP → HF download issue (known flaky)
Spot preemption on zero-shot jobs → retry via job_recovery

success-criteria.criterion: MLflow GCS artifact path works for all model sizes (68 MB to 900 MB)
Same-region europe-west4 eliminates all egress costs

focus-areas.area: Validate that file_mounts removal does not cause any data loss
Confirm SkyPilot version mismatch has no functional impact
Identify any new failure patterns not seen in passes 9-10
Measure actual duration per model to calibrate future estimates
Verify GCS lifecycle policies work correctly for non-current objects

cloud-test-upgrade.current-state: 107 new tests this session. Staging: 6721, Prod: 7005.
cloud-test-upgrade.target-state: Add tests per cloud observation from this pass.

test-group.name: post-pass-11-observations
test-group.test: GCS artifact size verification: checkpoint in MLflow matches expected model size
Same-region latency: GAR image pull time below 5 minutes
Duration calibration: actual vs expected per model type
Any new failure pattern discovered during this pass

cost-summary.comparison: 10th pass wasted $23.30 with zero useful output
cost-summary.always-on-infra-cost-during-experiment: Cloud SQL (db-g1-small): ~$0.78/day Cloud Run (MLflow, min 1): ~$0.20/day SkyPilot Controller (n4-standard-4): ~$3.22/day Total: ~$4.20/day (~EUR 3.87/day)

resource.name: SkyPilot Controller
resource.note: Orchestrates only — training VMs are in europe-west4

resource.name: FinOps Report
resource.note: Complete GCP cost baseline. Verify no new unexpected charges after experiment.
